title: Citrusy Smoky Tunisian Chicken Tagine
description: A slow‑cooked, creamy chicken tagine bursting with smoked paprika, Sichuan pepper, and zesty orange peel, perfect for a post‑workout dinner. It’s nut‑free and pairs wonderfully with couscous.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 150 °C (300 °F). Season the chicken thighs with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a Dutch oven over medium heat. Sear the chicken until golden brown on both sides, about 3–4 minutes each; remove and set aside.
In the same pot, sauté the chopped onion until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic, smoked paprika, cumin, and crushed Sichuan peppercorns; cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in the canned tomatoes, dried orange peel, lemon juice, and honey, breaking up the tomatoes with a spoon.
Return the chicken to the pot, pour in the chicken broth, and bring to a gentle simmer.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven. Cook slowly for 80 minutes until the chicken is tender and juices have thickened.
Remove the pot from the oven. Stir in the Greek yogurt until the sauce is creamy and smooth; adjust seasoning with salt and pepper if needed.
Let the tagine rest for 5 minutes, then ladle over the chicken. Garnish with chopped parsley before serving.
Serve hot with a side of steamed couscous or crusty bread.
